ConstructorParams.summary: Initializes an object instance with the supplied values.

ChangesFilespec.summary: Gets or sets the changes file name.

remarks.para:
This is the name of a file that contains the backup "copy" and "delete" commands.
These commands can be viewed and edited before running the code to apply these changes.

IncludeFilters.summary: Gets or sets the included file filters.

remarks.para: A list of file patterns using wild cards separated with the "Vertical Bar" (pipe) character. For example: "*.cs|*.sln|*.csproj |*.cmd"
Files that match one of these file patterns will be included in the Code Backup.
Files will be copied to the target folder if they do not exist there or the source file has changed.

Files will be deleted from the target folder if they do not exist in the source folder. This keeps the folders synched.
However, this behavior must be managed carefully to prevent losing code.

IncludeMissingTargetFolders.summary: Gets or sets the include missing target folders flag.

remarks.para: The standard behavior is to only copy files where the target folder already exists.
The standard behavior uses this implicit way of controlling which folders get backed up instead of having to explicitly list the source folders to be included.
When a new source folder is created, it must be copied to or created in the target codeline to be included in the backup.
Setting the IncludeMissingTargetFolders property to true overrides the standard behavior and will create missing target folders.

Name.summary: Gets or sets the profile name.

SkipFiles.summary: Gets or sets the skip file list.

remarks.para: A list of file patterns using wild cards separated with the "Vertical Bar" (pipe) character. For example: "Build*.cmd|Example*.cs"
Files that match one of these file patterns will not be included in the Code Backup.

SkipFoldersFilespec.summary: Gets or sets the skipped folder file name.

remarks.para: This is the name of a file that contains partial target folder paths for folders that are intentionally excluded.

The backup program writes a partial target folder path to the file "MissingFolders.txt" when the backup detects a missing target folder.
This is notification of a source folder that is not included in the backup.

Adding a partial folder to the SkipFolders file prevents getting a missing folders entry for a source folder you intended to exclude.
This makes it easier to see the folders you did not intend to exclude.

SourceCodeline.summary: Gets or sets the source codeline value.

remarks.para: The source codeline is the name of the source folder that holds the code specific to that codeline.
A codeline contains the solutions, projects and other code that is a version of a framework, product or other associated code.

SourceRoot.summary: Gets or sets the source root value.

remarks.para: The source root is the full path to the source folder that contains the source codeline folders.

TargetCodeline.summary: Gets or sets the target codeline value.

remarks.para: The target codeline is the name of the target folder that holds the code specific to that codeline.
A codeline contains the solutions, projects and other code that is a version of a framework, product or other associated code.

TargetRoot.summary: Gets or sets the target root value.

remarks.para: The target root is the full path to the target folder that contains the target codeline folders.

SourcePath.remarks: The SourcePath is the full path combination of the SourceRoot and SourceCodeline.

TargetPath.remarks: The TargetPath is the full path combination of the TargetRoot and TargetCodeline.
